metadata.title: States PCS: Assam GK Questions for Assam Public Service Commission Exams

questions.question: Which Ahom king established Gargaon as the capital in 1540?
questions.explanation: Gargaon was established as the Ahom capital in 1540 by King Suklenmung. It had stone and wood structures and was fortified with a 5 km brick wall. The palace was rebuilt in 1752 by King Rajeswar Singha. The Ahom kingdom was located in Assam, Northeast India.

questions.question: Which Ahom king introduced Durga Puja with earthen idols in Assam?
questions.explanation: Swargadeo Susenghphaa (Pratap Singha), the 17th Ahom king (c. 1545–1641), introduced Durga Puja using earthen idols in Assam. He expanded the kingdom westward and established administrative offices such as Borbarua and Borphukan.

questions.question: Who was Premier of Ahom Kingdom (1662-1679) known for guerrilla warfare?
questions.explanation: Bahgaria Atan Burhagohain served as Premier of the Ahom Kingdom from 1662 to 1679. He led the northern army in the Battle of Saraighat and was known for guerrilla warfare and military engineering. He refused the Ahom throne twice and reorganized the Kamrup administration.

questions.question: Which fort did the Ahom Kingdom capture in the 1682 Battle of Itakhuli?
questions.explanation: The Ahom Kingdom captured Itakhuli Fort in 1682 during their battle against the Mughal Empire. After the victory, they regained control of Sarkar Kamrup and established administration at Guwahati under the Borphukan. Itakhuli Fort is located in Assam and was strategically significant in this conflict.

questions.question: Which multicaste gold-washing guild (khel) in the Ahom Kingdom included Kacharis, Bihiyas, and Koch?
questions.explanation: Sonowal was a multicaste gold-washing guild in the Ahom Kingdom comprising Kacharis, Bihiyas, Koch, Miri, Dafla, and Keot. The Kachari Sonowals are today recognized as a Scheduled Tribe in Assam.

questions.question: In which year did the Patharughat Raij-Mel protest against revenue hikes occur in Assam?
questions.explanation: The Patharughat Raij-Mel protest took place in 1894 in Assam against land revenue hikes. It resulted in a violent clash with British police forces during the colonial period.

questions.question: Who proposed merging entire Assam with Bengal to form Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Saadullah was a Muslim League leader in Assam who stated Muslims were majority in Assam. He proposed merging all of Assam with Bengal to form Pakistan, not only the Sylhet region. This proposal was part of the political negotiations preceding the partition of India in 1947.

questions.question: How many seats did Assam get in the Eastern Bengal and Assam Legislative Council after Morley-Minto Reforms?
questions.explanation: The Morley-Minto Reforms led to the Indian Councils Act 1909, which expanded legislative councils. The Eastern Bengal and Assam Legislative Council consisted of 41 members, including Europeans and native representatives. Assam was allocated 5 seats in this council to represent its population and interests under the new electoral system.

questions.question: How many manmade reservoirs are there in Assam?
questions.explanation: Assam has 10 manmade reservoirs covering 2662.5 hectares. These reservoirs support hydroelectric power, irrigation, and flood control projects within the state.

questions.question: What is the total cultivable area of Assam in lakh hectares?
questions.explanation: Assam’s total cultivable area is approximately 74 lakh hectares. The state has an ultimate irrigation potential of 27 lakh hectares. Assam is known for its agriculture-based economy with diverse cropping patterns.
